zh.Blore, Staffordshire, England: 英國英格蘭斯塔福爾布洛爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Savage, Lady Audley於1405年出生在英國爾時，她的父親John Savage26歲，母親Mrs John Savage24歲。她於1418年4月6日與Sir John Dutton在英國英格蘭爾結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1463年6月29日在英國爾達頓享年58歲。

zh-hans.Blore, Staffordshire, England: 英国英格兰斯塔福爾布洛爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Savage, Lady Audley于1405年出生在英国爾时，她的父亲John Savage26岁，母亲Mrs John Savage24岁。她于1418年4月6日与Sir John Dutton在英国英格兰爾结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1463年6月29日在英国爾達頓享年58岁。

nameMeanings.name: Savage,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a nickname for a wild or uncouth person, from Middle English and Old French salvage, sauvage ‘wild, untamed’ (from Late Latin salvaticus, literally ‘man of the woods’, a derivative of Latin silva ‘wood’ influenced by Latin salvus ‘whole’, i.e. natural). Compare French Sauvage .

Irish (Down): generally of English origin (it was taken to County Down in the 12th century), this name has also sometimes been adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Ó Sabháin, the name of a small south Munster sept, which was earlier Anglicized as O'Savin (see Savin ).
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic) Savich and of Serbian Savić (see Savic ).

variants.name: Savitt
meanings: Possibly English or Irish: variant of   Savage  . This surname is very rare in Britain.
Alternatively, perhaps an Americanized form of Jewish   Savich   or an American shortened and altered form of Jewish   Savitski  .

variants.name: Sauvage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel, Armand, Jean-Jacques.

French and Channel Islands: nickname for a wild or uncouth person, from Old French salvage, sauvage ‘untamed’ (from Late Latin salvaticus, a derivative of Latin silva ‘wood’, influenced by Latin salvus ‘whole’, i.e. natural). This surname is also found in England.
History: Jean Sauvage from Plouëc-du-Trieux in Côtes-d'Armor, Brittany (France), married Marguerite Martin in Les Cèdres, QC, in 1782.

variants.name: Salvage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Monique.
English (Sussex): variant of   Savage  .
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

variants.name: Ramage
meanings: Scottish and English (of Norman origin); French: nickname for a savage or unpredictable individual, from Old French ramage ‘wild, untamed’. The word is recorded once in Middle English with the sense ‘courageous’ and in Scots (spelled rammage) as ‘rash, furious’. Compare Savage .

variants.name: Sabin
meanings: English (Warwickshire and Northamptonshire): from the Middle English personal name Sabin (from Latin Sabinus; see 2 below) or its female equivalent Sabine (from Latin Sabina). In medieval England the feminine form was always more popular as a personal name.

French: from the Old French personal name Sabin, from Latin Sabinus. The name was originally referred to the Sabines, an ancient Italic people of central Italy whose name is of uncertain origin. In the 8th century BC the Romans slaughtered the Sabine menfolk and carried off the women. More influential as far as name-giving is concerned was the existence of several early Christian saints named Sabinus.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Sabháin ‘descendant of Sabhán’, a personal name based on sabh ‘cub’. As an Irish surname, this has mostly been absorbed into Savage .

variants.name: Selvidge
meanings: English (Leicestershire):
from Middle English selvage, silvadge ‘edge of a cloth, bandage, ornamental edging (to a cloth or garment)’, hence a nickname for someone who made or sold such items.
variant of   Savage  .

variants.name: Savidge
meanings: English: variant of   Savage  .

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Polish: variant of   Lada  .
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Andrzej, Grzegorz, Irena, Lucyna, Witold.

Polish (mainly Łada) and Czech (Moravian): from a short form of the Polish personal name Ładysław, Czech Ladislav, both from Vladislav (Latinized as Ladislaus), a Slavic name composed of the elements vlad ‘to rule’ (from Old Slavic vlasti, later vladati) + slav ‘glory’ or ‘famous’ (from slava ‘glory’). Compare 2 below.
Hungarian: from Lad(a), a short form of the personal name Ladomér, a Hungarian form of Slavic Vladimir (see Lado 2, compare 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Audley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Audley in Staffordshire, named from the Old English female personal name Aldgȳth + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Savage, Lady Audley was born in 1405, in Cheshire, England, her father, John Savage, was 26 and her mother, Mrs John Savage, was 24. She married Sir John Dutton on 6 April 1418, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 29 June 1463, in Dutton, Cheshire, England, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Savage, Lady Audley est née en 1405, à Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, John Savage, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Mrs John Savage, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Sir John Dutton le 6 avril 1418, à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 29 juin 1463, à Dutton, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 58 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Savage, Lady Audley 1405 in Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Savage 26 und ihre Mutter Mrs John Savage 24. Sie hat am 6. April 1418 in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Sir John Dutton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 29. Juni 1463 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Dutton, Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Savage, Lady Audley è nata il nel 1405 nel Cheshire, England, sua padre, John Savage, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Mrs John Savage, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Sir John Dutton il 6 aprile 1418, nel Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 29 giugno 1463, nel Dutton, Cheshire, England, all'età di 58 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Savage, Lady Audleyが1405年にイギリスチェシュアイアで生まれた時，父John Savageは26歳，母Mrs John Savageは24歳であった。1418年4月6日にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアでSir John Duttonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1463年6月29日にイギリスチェシュアイアダットオンで58歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Savage, Lady Audley(이)가 1405년 영국 체실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Savage(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs John Savage(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1418년 4월 6일 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에서 Sir John Dutton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1463년 6월 29일 영국 체실르 드턴에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Savage, Lady Audley nasceu em 1405, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Savage, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Mrs John Savage, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir John Dutton em 6 de abril de 1418, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 29 de junho de 1463, em Dutton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 58 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Savage, Lady Audley родилась в 1405 г. в местности чешир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Savage было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs John Savage, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir John Dutton 6 апреля 1418 г. в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 29 июня 1463 г. в местности дуттон, чешир, Англия в возрасте 58 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Savage, Lady Audley nació en 1405, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Savage, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Mrs John Savage, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Sir John Dutton el 6 de abril de 1418, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 29 de junio de 1463, en Dutton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 58 años.
